bug_summary: SessionEventLog::append assigned event ids via a lock-free atomic and then wrote to disk and pushed to memory under separately-acquired locks, so a normal append could reserve id N while a concurrent append_command reserved/wrote/pushed id N+1 first, leaving on-disk JSONL order and in-memory snapshot order out of event-id order.

cases.title: SessionEventLog::append reserves id outside the disk-writer lock, racing the ordered command-append path
cases.instructions: Review this diff. Return only the JSON object requested by the system prompt.

cases.title: Append-only log reserves sequence id outside the mutex, so disk/slice order can drift from id order
cases.instructions: Review this diff. Return only the JSON object requested by the system prompt.
cases.diff:
diff --git a/eventlog/eventlog.go b/eventlog/eventlog.go
new file mode 100644
--- /dev/null
+++ b/eventlog/eventlog.go
@@ -0,0 +1,44 @@
+package eventlog
+
+import (
+	"fmt"
+	"os"
+	"sync"
+	"sync/atomic"
+)
+
+// Log is an append-only event log mirrored to a JSONL-ish file. Each
+// record gets a monotonic id; consumers replay the file and the
+// in-memory slice and assume both are sorted by id.
+type Log struct {
+	mu     sync.Mutex
+	nextID atomic.Uint64
+	file   *os.File
+	events []string
+}
+
+// AppendCommand is the strict path: it holds mu across id assignment,
+// the disk write, and the in-memory push, so file order and slice
+// order always match id order.
+func (l *Log) AppendCommand(payload string) uint64 {
+	l.mu.Lock()
+	defer l.mu.Unlock()
+	id := l.nextID.Add(1)
+	line := fmt.Sprintf("%d:%s\n", id, payload)
+	_, _ = l.file.WriteString(line)
+	l.events = append(l.events, line)
+	return id
+}
+
+// Append is the best-effort path. It reserves an id WITHOUT the lock,
+// then takes the lock separately to write and push.
+func (l *Log) Append(payload string) uint64 {
+	id := l.nextID.Add(1)
+	line := fmt.Sprintf("%d:%s\n", id, payload)
+	l.mu.Lock()
+	_, _ = l.file.WriteString(line)
+	l.events = append(l.events, line)
+	l.mu.Unlock()
+	return id
+}
